title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dermatitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dermatitis are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to treat various skin conditions, including dermatitis. They are a popular alternative to over-the-counter and prescription creams due to their natural composition and minimal side effects. Some examples of herbal tinctures that help with dermatitis include calendula, chamomile, and witch hazel. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing properties that reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by dermatitis. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their skincare routine, individuals with dermatitis can experience relief from their symptoms, improved skin texture, and an overall enhancement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, irritation, and infection in the affected areas. Its active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, help to soothe irritated skin, decrease inflammation, and promote healing. Yarrow tincture can also stimulate circulation, which aids in the removal of toxins and waste products from the skin, further supporting the healing process. Additionally, its astringent properties can help to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tincture, attributed to volatile oils like azulene, helps prevent skin infections and supports dermatitis healing by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, owing to tannins and resins, helps tighten and protect skin, reducing skin irritation and inflammation in dermatitis.

Wound-healing: The wound-healing property of yarrow tincture, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and astringent effects, promotes tissue regeneration and helps alleviate symptoms of dermatitis.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic property, resulting from compounds like salicylic acid, helps relieve pain and itching, providing comfort and relief for individuals with dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and anti-pruritic properties, making them useful for soothing irritated skin in dermatitis.
Flowers: Rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory and wound-healing effects, aiding in the treatment of dermatitis.
Stems: Contain therapeutic compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, that help reduce redness and irritation in dermatitis.
Seeds: Provide essential fatty acids and antioxidants, promoting skin barrier repair and alleviating symptoms of dermatitis.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agents, such as achilleine and sternbin, beneficial for managing dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning when they are fully open.
Combine one part of the herb material with two parts of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, squeezing the herbal material to extract every last drop.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains flavonoids, carotenoids, and essential oils that provide these benefits. These components help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis. Pot marigold tincture can also promote tissue regeneration, which may help in the faster healing of dermatitis-related lesions. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties can prevent secondary infections, providing additional relief to those suffering from dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dermatitis as it is often characterized by red, swollen, and painful skin. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the flavonoids and triterpenes present in the plant.

Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for dermatitis as it can help soothe broken, itchy, or irritated skin. This is due to the presence of the plant's flavonoids, carotenoids, and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture has antimicrobial properties, which are crucial for dermatitis as it can help prevent infections that may occur due to broken skin. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the plant's essential oils and alkaloids.

Antipruritic: The tincture can alleviate itching, which is a common symptom of dermatitis. This is important as constant scratching can worsen the condition and lead to further skin damage. The antipruritic effect is due to the presence of the plant's flavonoids and essential oils.

Immunomodulatory: Pot marigold tincture helps regulate the immune system, which is important for dermatitis as it often involves an overactive immune response. This is due to the plant's polysaccharides and saponins, which have immunomodulatory effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them ideal for soothing irritated skin in dermatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain high levels of antioxidants and have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating skin irritation and redness associated with dermatitis.

Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of the pot marigold plant still contain beneficial compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making them a useful addition to medicinal tinctures for dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula flowers, ideally in their peak potency and freshness.
Combine 200g of calendula flowers with 700ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Store the calendula tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, using within one year of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation, making it an effective treatment for dermatitis symptoms. Additionally, chamomile has been found to possess antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in damaged skin. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate itching and discomfort associated with dermatitis. Furthermore, chamomile is a natural and gentle alternative to harsher chemical treatments, making it a suitable option for those with sensitive skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its high content of bisabolol and chamazulene, helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property, owing to the presence of active compounds like alpha-bisabolol and matricin, helps prevent skin infections that can worsen dermatitis symptoms.
Wound healing: The wound healing property of German chamomile tincture, attributed to its flavonoids and essential oils, promotes tissue regeneration and helps repair dermatitis-damaged skin.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property, provided by the high levels of terpenoids and flavonoids, helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate dermatitis.

Anti-pruritic: German chamomile tincture's anti-pruritic property, resulting from its bisabolol and azulene content, alleviates itching and reduces the urge to scratch, helping manage dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: German chamomile flowers are rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antiphlogistic properties, making them ideal for soothing irritated skin in dermatitis.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain flavonoids and essential oils that have a calming effect on the skin, aiding in the treatment of dermatitis.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and support skin barrier function, beneficial for dermatitis management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and measure out 100 grams for extraction.
Combine the measured flowers with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label with the date prepared and ingredients used, ready for use within two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb's mucilage and tannins can soothe and protect irritated skin, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort. Plantain's allantoin content promotes skin cell regeneration, which may help in the healing process of dermatitis lesions. Its ability to inhibit histamine release can also alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, a common cause of dermatitis. Overall, plantain tincture provides a natural and holistic approach to managing dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in dermatitis, soothing the skin and alleviating redness and swelling, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin and catalpol.
Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing in dermatitis, facilitating skin repair and tissue regeneration, owing to the allantoin and rosmarinic acid content.

Antioxidant: Plantain tincture has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms, thanks to the high levels of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of secondary infections in dermatitis, attributable to its antibacterial and antifungal compounds, like aucubin, ursolic acid, and tannins.

Immune-modulating: Plantain tincture helps regulate the immune response, minimizing the overreaction that can lead to dermatitis symptoms, by influencing the release of cytokines and other immune factors, due to the polysaccharides, flavonoids, and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing irritated skin in dermatitis.
Seeds: Rich in mucilage, seeds can help reduce inflammation and provide a protective barrier for the skin.
Bark: Has astringent properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in dermatitis.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help manage symptoms of dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and clean them thoroughly by rinsing with cold running water.
Chop the cleaned leaves into small pieces and combine with vodka or a similar solvent in a ratio of 1:3.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from solids and discard solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with dermatitis because it contains allantoin, a substance that promotes the growth of new skin cells and helps to heal wounds. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis. Moreover, comfrey tincture has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, providing relief from the discomfort caused by dermatitis. Its ability to moisturize and hydrate the skin can help to alleviate the dryness and flakiness associated with this skin condition. Overall, comfrey tincture can help to promote the healing of dermatitis, reduce its symptoms, and improve the overall health and appearance of the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, helps reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis.
Wound healing: Comfrey tincture promotes wound healing, thanks to allantoin and tannins, which accelerate skin cell regeneration and help alleviate dermatitis-related skin damage.
Pain relief: The analgesic effect of comfrey tincture, attributed to pyrrolizidine alkaloids, helps alleviate itching, pain, and discomfort caused by dermatitis.
Antioxidant: Comfrey tincture's antioxidant properties, mainly from rosmarinic acid and phenolic acids, protect skin cells from oxidative stress and aid in soothing dermatitis-prone skin.
Moisturizing: Comfrey tincture's moisturizing effect, due to mucilaginous polysaccharides, helps maintain skin hydration, reducing dryness and scaling associated with dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in allantoin, a compound that promotes wound healing and skin regeneration.
Rhyzomes: Contain rosmarinic acid, an anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce redness and itching in dermatitis.
Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties beneficial for dermatitis.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, aiding in the treatment of dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems in abundance at peak growth stage for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested plant material thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt debris and impurities.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter to facilitate extraction.
Steep the chopped plant material in high-proof vodka or neutral grain spirit at a 1:5 ratio for 2 weeks to extract active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for long-term preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and luteolin, have been shown to help decrease inflammation and promote skin repair. Additionally, dandelion is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, which are essential for maintaining healthy skin and boosting the immune system. These nutrients can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, which can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms. By promoting skin health and reducing inflammation, dandelion tincture can provide relief and improve the overall appearance of skin affected by dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis, due to the presence of flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in dandelion tincture, including vitamins A, C, and E, helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms.

Wound-healing: Dandelion tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is beneficial for dermatitis as it can help repair the skin barrier and alleviate symptoms, thanks to active compounds like taraxasterol and β-sitosterol.

Immune-modulating: Dandelion tincture can help regulate the immune response, reducing the overreaction that can lead to dermatitis symptoms, due to the presence of polysaccharides and sesquiterpene lactones.

Detoxification: Dandelion tincture supports the liver and kidneys, helping the body eliminate toxins and waste products that can trigger or worsen dermatitis, owing to the diuretic properties of taraxacin and taraxacerin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties, making them useful for treating dermatitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-itching properties, helping to alleviate dermatitis symptoms.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, beneficial for soothing dermatitis.
Buds: Contain high levels of taraxasterol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, useful for treating dermatitis.
Seeds: Rich in essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dandelion roots and leaves by cutting them in early morning dew for best flavor.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned dandelion roots and leaves into small pieces and combine them in a clean glass jar.
Fill a 100-proof vodka or everclear with the chopped plant material leaving about an inch of headspace at top.
Steep the mixture for four weeks in a cool dark place shaking jar daily to extract maximum flavor and nutrients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with dermatitis because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can soothe irritated skin and promote healing. Burdock contains essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and moisturize the skin, helping to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation. Its detoxifying properties can also help eliminate toxins from the body that can contribute to skin conditions like dermatitis. By boosting the immune system, burdock tincture can help the body fight off infections and promote overall skin health. Regular use of burdock tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of dermatitis and improve the appearance of the skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dermatitis as it can alleviate redness, swelling, and itching. This property is attributed to the presence of polyacetylenes and tannins.

Antimicrobial: Burdock tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms, which is crucial for dermatitis as it can prevent secondary infections. The antimicrobial effect is due to the presence of phenolic acids and quinic acid derivatives.

Diuretic: Burdock tincture promotes the elimination of fluids and toxins from the body, which is significant for dermatitis as it can help reduce skin inflammation and irritation. The diuretic effect is attributed to the presence of arctiin and arctigenin.

Antioxidant: Burdock tincture neutralizes free radicals and protects the skin from oxidative stress, which is essential for dermatitis as it can promote skin healing and regeneration. The antioxidant effect is due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and quercetin.

Blood purifier: Burdock tincture helps eliminate toxins from the blood, which is important for dermatitis as it can reduce skin inflammation and irritation. The blood purifying effect is attributed to the presence of arctiopicrin and lignans.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe skin irritation and reduce redness associated with dermatitis.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their demulcent and vulnerary properties, providing relief from skin inflammation and promoting skin repair.
Flowers: Burdock flowers contain flavonoids and tannins that have anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, making them useful for treating skin conditions like dermatitis.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in essential fatty acids and phytosterols, which can help reduce inflammation, moisturize the skin, and support the overall health of the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried burdock root arctium lappa from sustainable sources.
Chop the root into small pieces then macerate in high-proof vodka 25% abv for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and squeeze out as much liquid as possible retaining solids.
Combine the remaining liquid with a 1:1 ratio of pure water and bring to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Filter the extract using activated charcoal and bottle in dark glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tincture helps with dermatitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can reduce skin inflammation, redness, and itching associated with dermatitis. The herb's active compounds, including lectins, flavonoids, and polyphenols, have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other inflammatory chemicals in the body. Moreover, stinging nettle has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections commonly associated with dermatitis. By alleviating these symptoms, stinging nettle tincture can promote skin healing and provide relief to individuals suffering from dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for dermatitis as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and itching. The anti-inflammatory properties come from compounds like lectins, flavonoids, and lignans.

Antihistamine: Nettle tincture has natural antihistamine properties, which can help with dermatitis by reducing histamine-related symptoms such as itching and inflammation. The antihistamine effect is attributed to the presence of histamine, serotonin, and acetylcholine in nettle.

Antipruritic: Stinging nettle tincture can help relieve itching, a common symptom of dermatitis, due to its antipruritic properties. Active constituents responsible for this effect include histamine, serotonin, and various polysaccharides.

Antioxidant: Nettle tincture contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is beneficial for dermatitis. Key antioxidants in nettle include vitamins A, C, and K, as well as various flavonoids and carotenoids.

Immune-modulating: Stinging nettle tincture can help regulate immune responses, which is important for dermatitis as it may help control the overactive immune response that contributes to the condition. The immune-modulating properties of nettle are attributed to polysaccharides, lectins, and various sterols.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the stinging nettle plant are rich in histamine, which can help reduce the inflammation associated with dermatitis.
Stems: The stems of the nettle plant contain high levels of chlorophyll and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing irritated skin.

Flowers: The flowers of the stinging nettle plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating skin conditions such as dermatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the stinging nettle plant are rich in fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin.

Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain a variety of compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating skin conditions such as dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves by cutting 50-75 grams of leafy stems in the early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves in small bunches or spread them out thin on paper towels within 24 hours to prevent mold.
Combine 200 grams of dried nettle leaves with 400 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are completely covered by liquid.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the mixture every three days to enhance extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with dermatitis because it contains properties that are beneficial for the skin. It has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis. Witch hazel also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, it can help soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from the itching and discomfort associated with dermatitis. Its natural properties make it a gentle and effective option for those looking for a more natural approach to managing their dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dermatitis, due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help dry up weeping sores and oozing blisters, thanks to the high concentration of tannins.
Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture's antioxidant properties protect skin from free radical damage, promoting skin health and healing, owing to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent skin infections and promote wound healing, as a result of the tannins and volatile oils.
Soothing: Witch hazel tincture's soothing properties provide relief from itching and discomfort, due to the presence of gallic acid and eugenol.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains high levels of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe and heal dermatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness, itching, and irritation associated with dermatitis.
Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant contain active compounds such as gallic acid and essential oils, which have antiseptic and astringent properties, helpful in treating dermatitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for soothing and healing irritated skin caused by dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana twigs and leaves from the wild or cultivated garden.
Clean and chop the plant material into small pieces, discarding any debris or damaged parts.
Combine one part chopped plant material with two parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow the tincture to macerate.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses. These properties make St. John's wort tincture effective in soothing irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting the healing of dermatitis lesions. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture may help alleviate the psychological stress and anxiety often associated with skin conditions, further supporting the healing process. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using St. John's wort tincture or any other herbal remedy, especially if you are taking other medications or have a medical condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for dermatitis as it is often characterized by inflamed, red, and itchy skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: The tincture helps fight off microorganisms, preventing skin infections, which is crucial for dermatitis since the skin's barrier function is often compromised. The main antimicrobial constituents are hyperforin, hypericin, and flavonoids.

Wound healing: St. John's wort tincture promotes wound healing, which is significant for dermatitis as the skin can be broken or have open sores. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and tannins.

Analgesic: The tincture helps alleviate pain, which is important for dermatitis as the condition can cause discomfort and itching. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tincture has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to the overall skin health in dermatitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds such as hypericin and hyperforin, St. John's Wort flowers are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain similar active compounds as the flowers, making them suitable for treating skin conditions like dermatitis, as they help reduce inflammation and fight infection.

Stems: Although less potent than flowers and leaves, St. John's Wort stems still contain active compounds that can contribute to the overall effectiveness of medicinal tinctures for dermatitis, aiding in skin repair and regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers at dawn on a sunny day weighing about 2 ounces.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces and combine with vodka at a ratio of 1:3 for soaking.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing the herbal material to steep.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeezing out as much tincture as possible from the solids discarding any remaining plant matter.
Bottle the resulting st john's wort tincture in amber glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years using it within the recommended dosages.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dermatitis with herbal tinctures is by using a proven regimen of natural remedies. For instance, calendula tincture, known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, can alleviate itching and irritation. Chamomile tincture, rich in antioxidants, reduces redness and inflammation. Apply these tinctures topically, diluted with a carrier oil, two to three times a day. Additionally, consider introducing milk thistle or burdock tinctures orally to support skin health and detoxification from within. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dermatitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema and psoriasis. Eczema is a chronic skin condition characterized by inflammation, redness, and itchiness, while psoriasis is an autoimmune condition that causes skin cells to build up rapidly, resulting in scaly, red patches. Herbal tinctures, such as chamomile, calendula, and burdock, can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by reducing inflammation, soothing irritation, and promoting skin healing. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
